result.book_thesis: To fulfill the American experiment's founding ideals of equity and civic unity, public schools must replace the scientifically flawed 'skills-based' educational model with a specific, common subject-matter curriculum in the early grades to create a shared public sphere.

core_frameworks.name: The Common School Ideal
core_frameworks.description: An educational model designed to create a transethnic civic core that allows individuals from diverse backgrounds to participate as equals in the public sphere.

components.name: Civic Core
components.description: A shared body of knowledge and values that enables political and social unity across different ethnicities.

components.name: Public Sphere Participation
components.description: The ability of all citizens to engage in national discourse and economic life regardless of their private origin.

core_frameworks.name: The How-To Conception of Schooling
core_frameworks.description: The dominant 20th-century educational theory that emphasizes abstract skills like 'critical thinking' and 'reading strategies' over specific subject-matter content.

components.name: Skill Transcendence
components.description: The belief that thinking skills can be taught independently of factual knowledge.

components.name: Content Neutrality
components.description: The rejection of a specific, grade-by-grade subject-matter curriculum.

chapter_previews.chapter: 1. The Inspiring Idea of the Common School
chapter_previews.predicted_main_argument: The American founders envisioned public education as the primary tool for perpetuating political institutions and ensuring the success of the American experiment.
The decline of American educational quality is directly linked to the 1930s rejection of specific subject-matter requirements.

chapter_previews.chapter: 3. Transethnic America and the Civic Core
chapter_previews.predicted_main_argument: A shared curriculum is the only mechanism that can effectively bridge ethnic and group differences to create a unified American identity.

chapter_previews.chapter: 4. Linguistic America and the Public Sphere
chapter_previews.predicted_main_argument: Literacy and effective communication in the public sphere require a foundation of shared background knowledge rather than just abstract linguistic skills.

chapter_previews.chapter: 5. Competence and Equality Narrowing the Two Achievement Gaps
chapter_previews.predicted_main_argument: Achievement gaps between demographic groups can only be closed by providing all students with the same high-quality core content in early grades.

chapter_previews.chapter: 6. Competence and Community Renewing Public Education
chapter_previews.predicted_main_argument: Restoring the common school model is essential for renewing the social bond and the efficacy of American democracy.

key_terms.definition: A character of a society that accommodates diverse groups and ethnicities in harmony without requiring the abandonment of private identities.
key_terms.source_passage: I stress that they have special aims also that arise from our country's transethnic character.

key_terms.definition: A philosophical concept describing a macro-society that functions as a harmonious collection of diverse sub-communities.
key_terms.source_passage: America remains the most successful experiment so far in creating what the philosopher John Rawls called 'a social union of social unions.'

key_terms.definition: The disparity in educational performance and literacy rates between different demographic and socioeconomic groups.
key_terms.source_passage: It concerns itself... with overcoming low literacy rates and narrowing the achievement gaps between demographic groups.

key_terms.definition: The shared space of national life, commerce, and politics where citizens participate as equals based on their virtues and talents.
key_terms.source_passage: it is the institution that prepares children to participate effectively in the common public sphere.

intellectual_genealogy.name: Albert Shanker
intellectual_genealogy.contribution: A 'tough liberal' who championed the common school, assimilation, and content-based education to overcome left-right polarization.

intellectual_genealogy.name: Thomas Jefferson
intellectual_genealogy.contribution: Provided the foundational idea that education should allow citizens to participate based on 'talents and virtues' rather than birthright.

intellectual_genealogy.name: Horace Mann
intellectual_genealogy.contribution: Propagandist for public education who argued that 'competence brings community' and that schooling should equalize opportunity.

intellectual_genealogy.name: John Rawls
intellectual_genealogy.contribution: Philosophical framework for a pluralistic society defined as a 'social union of social unions.'

intellectual_genealogy.name: Abraham Lincoln
intellectual_genealogy.contribution: Identified the role of civic education in the 'perpetuation of our political institutions.'
